sub_sections.title: Project's pathways towards impact
sub_sections.content:
MAESTRO makes a unique, demonstrable contribution toward the two expected outcomes and five expected impacts specified in the work programme. This section describes the contribution of this specific project — not AI agent R&I in general.

Contribution to expected outcomes. The call specifies two expected outcomes. EO-01 requires significant improvements in the autonomy, robustness, and reliability of AI agents through advanced planning mechanisms, memory management, and reasoning capabilities. MAESTRO addresses this through three interoperable architectural outputs. First, the ATU-led neuro-symbolic planning engine (WP2, D2-02, M36) integrates hierarchical task networks and Monte Carlo Tree Search with LLM-based heuristics and verifiable sub-task graph decompositions with formal pre- and post-conditions, advancing agent autonomy and robustness beyond current chain-of-thought approaches by enabling verifiable plan monitoring and self-correction (T2-03, WP2). Second, the BIIS-led adaptive tri-store memory architecture (WP3, D3-02, M30) combines working, episodic, and semantic memory stores with a learned controller, enabling agents to maintain coherence across extended interaction sequences without retraining — directly addressing the reliability failures arising from LLM context-window limitations in long-horizon deployments. Third, the FIIT-led evaluation framework and benchmark suite (WP8, D8-01 M12, D8-02 M42) produces standardised, reproducible baselines quantifying improvements in planning, memory, and multi-agent coordination across task categories, enabling the field to measure the improvements MAESTRO delivers. All three outputs are validated to TRL 5 across three sector-specific demonstrators (WP5, WP6, WP7). EO-02 requires innovative multi-agent frameworks demonstrating effective decentralised coordination and collaboration beyond individual agent capabilities. MAESTRO delivers this through the CERIA-led formally verified multi-agent coordination protocol suite (WP4, D4-01 M18, D4-02 M36), with mathematically proven convergence guarantees, auditable multi-agent interaction traces (T4-03), and formal conflict-resolution mechanisms under partial observability — validated across the healthcare (WP5), manufacturing (WP6), and logistics (WP7) demonstrators, confirming generalisability to heterogeneous real-world multi-agent deployments.

Contribution to expected impacts. MAESTRO addresses all five expected impacts.

EI-01 (contribution to the European Apply AI Strategy, advancing adoption in healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving): MAESTRO delivers three sector-validated TRL-5 demonstrators covering all three priority sectors. The NIHS-led clinical decision support system (WP5, D5-01 M18, D5-02 M42) deploys multi-specialist-agent coordination on 500 de-identified clinical cases with HL7 FHIR integration, producing auditable reasoning traces in alignment with EU AI Act high-risk system requirements, and targeting formal adoption planning with ≥3 European hospital networks by M48 (KPI-01). The ISIA-led manufacturing process optimisation demonstrator (WP6, D6-02 M42) validates multi-agent coordination with digital twin integration in an operational factory (T6-01 through T6-03), targeting ≥15% defect reduction and ≥10% energy savings versus pre-deployment baseline (KPI-02). The ELI-led logistics transfer demonstrator (WP7, D7-01 M24, D7-02 M42) validates MAESTRO architecture generalisation across three international supply chain corridors (T7-03) and produces a formal transferability mapping to in-vehicle autonomous driving, confirming coverage of all three Apply AI Strategy sectors and evidencing MAESTRO's architectural portability (KPI-03: ≥20% disruption recovery time improvement).

EI-02 (wider EDIH-mediated industrial deployment of next-generation AI agent solutions): MAESTRO operationalises three specific EDIH deployment channels. NIHS-led EDIH engagement targets ≥3 hospital networks with formal adoption planning agreements by M48, grounded in the D5-02 validated evaluation dataset. ISIA-led EIT Manufacturing and ≥2-EDIH technology transfer targets ≥5 manufacturing SMEs by M48 (KPI-04). BAL-led open-source ecosystem development (D9-02, WP9, T9-01) targets ≥10 European SMEs and startups actively building on MAESTRO by M48, with ≥3 EDIHs actively disseminating MAESTRO technology (KPI-05).

EI-03 (open scientific advancement through AI-on-demand, strengthening European AI research infrastructure): ATU, BIIS, CERIA, and FIIT target ≥20 peer-reviewed publications in NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, AAMAS, and AAAI, all open-access under Plan S-compliant CC-BY licence (KPI-06), targeting ≥100 aggregate citations within three years of publication (KPI-09). FIIT contributes the MAESTRO benchmark suite dataset (D8-02, WP8, T8-03) to the AI-on-demand platform, targeting adoption as evaluation infrastructure by ≥5 external research groups (KPI-07). BAL releases the open-source MAESTRO framework (D9-02, WP9, M36) on GitHub and the AI-on-demand platform, targeting ≥500 GitHub stars within six months of the M36 release (KPI-10).

EI-04 (European open strategic autonomy in digital and emerging enabling technologies — addressed as an inferred expected impact derived from topic destination placement; no explicit expected impact statement for strategic autonomy appears in the call scope or expected outcome sections of the source work programme document): The open, European-origin MAESTRO architecture, differentiated from non-European approaches by formal verifiability and auditability rather than parameter scale, provides sovereign European AI agent capabilities accessible without platform lock-in under Apache 2.0. CERIA's formal protocol specification (D4-01, WP4) serves as the basis for ≥1 standardisation submission to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE Standards Association by M48 (KPI-08), accompanied by an EU AI Act multi-agent transparency alignment report, extending MAESTRO's reach into international AI governance norm-setting.

EI-05 (unlocking economic potential of AI agents across diverse sectors including data analytics and coding): The MAESTRO planning engine (D2-02, WP2) enables verifiable goal decomposition making agentic workflows economically deployable in analytical and coding contexts where execution failures carry cost. The adaptive memory system (D3-02, WP3) enables long-horizon deployment in financial analysis, legal research, and software development without retraining. The external tool and API orchestration layer (OUT-9, D8-01, WP2/WP8) provides typed tool registry, formal tool selection policies, standardised API adapters, and post-invocation verification — prerequisites for economically viable agentic workflows across any sector. The benchmark suite (D8-02) and open-source framework (D9-02) make these advances accessible to European SMEs and startups (KPI-10: ≥500 GitHub stars M36+6 months; KPI-11: statistically significant improvement over SOTA baselines at p<0.05 across ≥3 task categories).

Target groups. Six specific target groups will benefit from MAESTRO's results: (1) European hospital network clinical informatics leads and health technology assessment bodies evaluating AI agent pathways for clinical decision support, reached via NIHS-led EDIH engagement (WP5, IMP-2); (2) European manufacturing SMEs and operators in process-intensive sectors evaluating AI-driven process optimisation, reached via ISIA-led EIT Manufacturing and EDIH technology transfer (WP6, IMP-3); (3) European logistics and supply chain operators seeking multi-actor coordination and disruption resilience, reached via ELI-led operational integration and exploitation roadmap (WP7, IMP-4); (4) European AI SMEs, startups, and independent software vendors seeking non-proprietary AI agent infrastructure, reached via BAL-led open-source ecosystem on GitHub and AI-on-demand (WP9, IMP-5); (5) European and international AI research groups requiring reproducible agent evaluation baselines, reached via FIIT-led benchmark suite and publication programme (WP8, IMP-1); (6) ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 and EU AI Act implementation officials engaged in multi-agent AI standardisation and transparency, reached via CERIA-led protocol specification and submissions (WP4, IMP-6).

Requirements and potential barriers beyond scope and duration. Four potential barriers are identified with corresponding mitigating measures. First, EU AI Act high-risk classification for clinical AI creates regulatory gating for hospital network deployment beyond project duration: mitigated proactively through auditable reasoning trace design and formal EU AI Act compliance evidence package in WP5 (D5-02), initiated from project start. Second, hospital procurement and health technology assessment cycles typically extend 18–36 months beyond technology validation, making post-project uptake uncertain: mitigated by initiating formal EDIH-mediated adoption planning from M36 and targeting signed adoption planning agreements with ≥3 hospital networks by M48 as a verified project KPI (KPI-01). Third, open-source community critical mass may not materialise without active facilitation: mitigated by concurrent AI-on-demand platform contribution, EDIH network amplification, and targeted benchmark adoption outreach targeting ≥5 external research groups by M48. Fourth, standardisation body comment-and-revision timelines operate independently of the project schedule: mitigated by initiating the ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE Standards Association submission process from M42, allowing time for the formal tracking confirmation to be received within project lifetime (KPI-08 target: M48).

sub_sections.title: Measures to maximise impact - Dissemination, exploitation and communication
sub_sections.content:
MAESTRO's dissemination and exploitation plan (DEP) coordinates nine dissemination and five exploitation activities across all eight consortium partners, calibrated to reach each target group through the channels most appropriate to that group. A detailed DEP will be produced as a distinct mandatory project deliverable within six months of grant signature (D9-01, WP9-BAL, M6), updated periodically throughout the project. This outline DEP constitutes the admissibility condition; D9-01 provides the full operational specification.

Dissemination activities.

Scientific publication and open access (ATU, BIIS, CERIA, FIIT; M12–M60): MAESTRO targets ≥20 peer-reviewed publications in top-tier AI research venues (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, AAMAS, AAAI), with an initial tranche from M12 covering WP2 planning engine and WP3 memory architecture early results. All publications will be deposited in Zenodo and partner institutional repositories under Plan S-compliant CC-BY licences within six months of acceptance, fulfilling the Horizon Europe open access mandate. Sensitive clinical data from WP5 will be anonymised in compliance with GDPR and EU AI Act requirements before any public release; the de-identified evaluation dataset D5-02 will be published as open data. ATU, CERIA, and FIIT pursue ≥3 invited keynotes at major AI conferences across M18–M60, coordinated under WP9 T9-03, targeting senior academic researchers and industrial AI practitioners who influence research directions and adoption decisions. This channel reaches the NeurIPS/ICML/ICLR/AAMAS audience most effectively through high-visibility in-person engagement.

Open-source framework release and AI-on-demand contribution (BAL; M36 and M48–M60): BAL releases the MAESTRO open-source framework (D9-02, M36) on GitHub and the AI-on-demand platform under Apache 2.0 licence, with documentation, tutorials, and the benchmark suite dataset (D8-02, M36). BAL's WP9 T9-01 manages community building, repository maintenance, and targeted outreach to European AI startup communities via EDIH network contacts. This channel reaches European AI SMEs and startups most effectively because it provides immediately usable, licenceable infrastructure without procurement barriers.

EDIH-mediated healthcare dissemination (NIHS; M36–M48): NIHS leads EDIH-facilitated engagement with European hospital network clinical informatics leads, using the validated clinical decision support evaluation dataset (D5-02, M42) and EU AI Act compliance evidence as the primary evidence package. NIHS's existing clinical domain relationships provide the trust context required for this segment. Target: ≥3 hospital networks with formal post-project adoption planning documented by M48.

EIT Manufacturing and EDIH industrial dissemination (ISIA; M36–M54): ISIA leads EIT Manufacturing network and ≥2-EDIH technology transfer, using the manufacturing process optimisation demonstrator software (D6-02, M42) and operational KPI evidence (≥15% defect reduction, ≥10% energy savings) as commercially tractable value propositions. Target: ≥5 European manufacturing SMEs engaged by M48.

Standardisation and policy dissemination (CERIA; M42–M48): CERIA submits the MAESTRO formally verified coordination protocol specification (D4-01) to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE Standards Association (M42–M48) and publishes an EU AI Act multi-agent transparency alignment report (M48). This channel reaches standardisation body members and EU AI Act policy officials who are responsible for multi-agent AI governance and for whom formal specifications constitute the relevant evidence format.

Exploitation activities.

EDIH-mediated clinical adoption exploitation (NIHS; M36–M48): NIHS produces formal post-project AI agent deployment adoption planning agreements with ≥3 European hospital networks, documenting a regulatory-compliant pathway for clinical decision support integration grounded in D5-02 validation evidence and EU AI Act high-risk system compliance documentation from WP5. These agreements establish a post-project clinical deployment pathway that bypasses the full procurement cycle through pre-established institutional relationships.

Manufacturing technology transfer via EDIH and EIT Manufacturing (ISIA; M36–M54): ISIA leads operational technology transfer of the manufacturing process optimisation demonstrator (D6-02, OUT-5) to ≥5 European manufacturing SMEs via ≥2 EDIH partnerships and the EIT Manufacturing network, establishing consulting and licensing revenue streams built around the demonstrator's measurable operational KPI outcomes.

Logistics operational system integration (ELI; M42–M48): ELI produces a confirmed integration roadmap for MAESTRO multi-agent coordination capabilities into its own operational supply chain management systems across ≥3 international corridors by M48, establishing a post-project commercial deployment track record and a reference for wider logistics sector adoption.

Open-source ecosystem and downstream commercial adoption (BAL; M36–M60): BAL leads ecosystem development targeting ≥10 European SMEs and startups with active MAESTRO-based production development by M48. The Apache 2.0 licence enables downstream commercial applications without licence restrictions. BAL maintains EDIH partnership agreements and produces the final exploitation and dissemination impact report (D9-03, M48) documenting adoption metrics.

Standardisation-driven protocol adoption (CERIA; M42–M54): CERIA targets adoption of the MAESTRO formally verified coordination protocol specification (D4-01) by ≥3 external multi-agent AI research projects as a reference architecture, with ≥1 formal ISO/IEC or IEEE submission received and tracked (KPI-08).

IPR management. MAESTRO adopts an open-core IPR strategy. The core framework, benchmark suite, evaluation infrastructure, and coordination protocol specifications are released under Apache 2.0, jointly owned by the consortium, to maximise European ecosystem adoption and lower barriers for SMEs and startups. Sector-specific demonstrator configurations, anonymised clinical validation datasets (GDPR-compliant), and industrial deployment parameters may be protected as trade secrets or licensed commercially by NIHS (clinical decision support configuration), ISIA (manufacturing process optimisation deployment), and ELI (logistics integration) respectively. BAL coordinates the consortium IP register under WP9 T9-02, ensuring that open-source release decisions are governed by the consortium agreement and do not unintentionally foreclose commercial licensing paths for any partner.

Communication activities. BAL leads public communication under WP9 T9-03 with three tiered objectives: (i) raise awareness across the European AI research community from M6 via project website and LinkedIn professional networks; (ii) engage industrial stakeholders in each target sector from M18 via targeted sector events and EDIH communication channels; (iii) create informed public awareness of trustworthy AI agent deployment by M36 through accessible project communication materials. ATU, CERIA, and FIIT's conference keynote programme (targeting ≥3 invited keynotes M18–M60) serves as the highest-visibility communication vehicle for the research and technical practitioner community.

Policy feedback. CERIA's standardisation submissions and EU AI Act multi-agent transparency alignment report (M48) generate direct, documented policy feedback contributing to the EU's multi-agent AI governance framework — specifically informing EU AI Act implementation guidance for multi-agent system transparency requirements. This feedback is disseminated through the MAESTRO final dissemination and impact report (D9-03, WP9-BAL, M48).

Post-project sustainability. MAESTRO's sustainability is grounded in three reinforcing mechanisms: the open-source MAESTRO framework community (dependent on reaching ≥10 SME/startup users by M48, creating self-sustaining community maintenance); operational embedding of demonstrators in live commercial environments at NIHS, ISIA, and ELI, generating post-project maintenance obligations driven by operational requirements rather than grant continuations; and CERIA's standardisation submissions embedding MAESTRO coordination protocol specifications as international normative references, independent of any single organisation's continued involvement. BAL coordinates the long-term exploitation strategy through WP9 and delivers the final exploitation handover documentation (D9-03, M48).

claim_statuses.claim_summary: EI-01 (Apply AI Strategy: healthcare, manufacturing, in-vehicle autonomous driving) addressed by PATH-EI-01 via WP5 clinical, WP6 manufacturing, and WP7 logistics demonstrators
EI-02 (EDIH-mediated industrial deployment) addressed by PATH-EI-02 via NIHS/ISIA/BAL EDIH channels targeting hospital networks, manufacturing SMEs, and open-source ecosystem
EI-03 (open scientific advancement through AI-on-demand) addressed by PATH-EI-03 via ≥20 publications, benchmark suite D8-02, open-source D9-02
EI-04 (European open strategic autonomy) addressed by PATH-EI-04 via open MAESTRO architecture, open-source Apache 2.0 release, and CERIA standardisation submission

Inference chain: topic placed in destination 'Achieving open strategic autonomy in digital and emerging enabling technologies (2026-27)' → advances in next-generation European AI agents serve EU strategic autonomy objectives in digital domain. Pathway substantively grounded in Tier 3 impacts.json IMP-1 and IMP-6.

claim_statuses.claim_summary: EI-05 (unlocking economic potential of AI agents across diverse sectors) addressed by PATH-EI-05 via planning engine, memory system, tool orchestration layer, and open-source framework

MAESTRO contributes to EO-01 (improvements in autonomy, robustness, reliability) via neuro-symbolic planning engine (WP2/D2-02, TRL 5), adaptive memory architecture (WP3/D3-02, TRL 5), and evaluation benchmark suite (WP8/D8-01, D8-02)

claim_statuses.claim_summary: MAESTRO contributes to EO-02 (innovative multi-agent frameworks for decentralised coordination) via formally verified coordination protocol suite (WP4/D4-01, D4-02) validated across WP5–WP7

claim_statuses.claim_summary: ≥3 hospital networks engaged in formal adoption planning by M48 (KPI-01, PATH-EI-01)
≥15% defect reduction and ≥10% energy savings in pilot factory (KPI-02, PATH-EI-01)
≥20% improvement in supply chain disruption recovery time across ≥3 international corridors (KPI-03, PATH-EI-01)
≥10 European SMEs/startups on MAESTRO framework and ≥3 EDIHs actively disseminating (KPI-04, KPI-05, PATH-EI-02)
≥20 peer-reviewed publications in top AI venues and ≥100 citations within three years (KPI-06, KPI-09, PATH-EI-03)
≥5 external research groups adopting MAESTRO benchmark suite (KPI-07, PATH-EI-03)
≥1 formal standardisation submission to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE by M48 (KPI-08, PATH-EI-04)

Post-project sustainability through three mechanisms: open-source community, operational commercial embedding, and international standardisation
